fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Pennsylvania Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Pennsylvania - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity pennsylvania, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to pennsylvania, speech-language pathologist compact pennsylvania, pennsylvania speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: State Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Pennsylvania Department of State, Bureau of Professional and Occupational Affairs

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Pennsylvania. Requires a master's degree, completed clinical fellowship (1,260+ hours), and a passing Praxis score.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their clinical fellowship. Allows supervised clinical practice while fulfilling the 1,260-hour experience requirement within two years.

steps.title: Apply through PALS Portal
steps.description: Submit the application through the Pennsylvania Licensing System (PALS). Pay the $50 application fee. Include official transcripts, Praxis scores, clinical fellowship verification, child abuse training certificate, and FBI fingerprint background check (effective July 7, 2025).

documents.detail: From an ASHA-accredited speech-language pathology program.
Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.
Documentation of 1,260+ hours of supervised professional experience with 80% direct client contact.
3 hours of Board-approved CE in child abuse recognition and reporting (required for initial licensure).
Required for new applicants effective July 7, 2025. Pre-register through IdentoGO with service code provided during application.

telepractice_text: Pennsylvania permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Pennsylvania SLP license to provide services to clients located in Pennsylvania. All telepractice services must meet the same standard of care as in-person services.
provisional_text: Pennsylvania issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their clinical fellowship. This allows supervised practice while fulfilling the 1,260-hour experience requirement. The supervising SLP must hold a current Pennsylvania license.
reciprocity_text: Pennsylvania does not have formal reciprocity agreements. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for a new Pennsylvania license through the PALS portal. ASHA CCC-SLP holders must still submit all required documentation, including transcripts, Praxis scores, and fellowship verification.

faqs.question: How much does a Pennsylvania SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: Pennsylvania has one of the lowest SLP licensing fees in the country. The initial application fee is $50. Biennial renewal is $65, due by July 31 of odd-numbered years.

faqs.question: What is the new fingerprinting requirement for Pennsylvania SLPs?
faqs.answer: Effective July 7, 2025, new health care practitioner applicants, including SLPs, must obtain a fingerprint-based FBI background check. Pre-register through IdentoGO using the service code provided during your application. This does not apply to renewals.

faqs.question: How many CE hours are required for Pennsylvania SLP renewal?
faqs.answer: Pennsylvania requires 20 hours of continuing education every two years, including 2 hours of mandated child abuse reporter training. CE credits must be through ASHA or ASHA-approved entities.

faqs.question: Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pennsylvania does not have formal reciprocity agreements. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for a new Pennsylvania license through the PALS portal. ASHA CCC-SLP holders must still submit all required documentation, including transcripts, Praxis scores, and fellowship verification.
